title: Protein Overnight Oats
metaDescription: Creamy protein overnight oats with 28g of protein per jar — no chalkiness, just Greek yogurt and cottage cheese. 5 minutes of prep, ready by morning.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: protein overnight oats, high protein oats, meal prep breakfast
ingredients: 1/2 cup milk (dairy or unsweetened almond milk)
Toppings: sliced banana, peanut butter, walnuts

steps.name: Blend the creamy base
steps.text: Add cottage cheese, Greek yogurt, milk, honey, and vanilla to a blender. Blend until completely smooth, about 20 seconds.

steps.name: Combine with oats
steps.text: Pour the blended mixture into a jar with the rolled oats and chia seeds. Stir well to combine.

steps.name: Refrigerate overnight
steps.text: Cover and refrigerate for at least 8 hours, or overnight. The oats will absorb the liquid and turn thick and creamy.

steps.name: Top and serve
steps.text: In the morning, stir once more and top with sliced banana, a swirl of peanut butter, and walnuts.

tips.question: Can I use protein powder instead of cottage cheese?
tips.answer: You can, but the texture and protein content here come from whole-food ingredients (cottage cheese + Greek yogurt), which most readers find creamier and less chalky than powder-based versions.

tips.question: How long do protein overnight oats keep in the fridge?
tips.answer: Up to 3-4 days in a sealed jar. Add fresh toppings just before eating rather than in advance.

tips.question: Can I make this dairy-free?
tips.answer: Swap the cottage cheese and Greek yogurt for a dairy-free yogurt alternative and unsweetened almond or oat milk — the protein content will be lower without the dairy base.

tips.question: Why blend the wet ingredients instead of just stirring?
tips.answer: Blending breaks down the cottage cheese curds completely, so the oats end up smooth and creamy instead of having pockets of cottage cheese texture.

images.heroAlt: Jar of protein overnight oats topped with banana slices, peanut butter and walnuts
images.textureAlt: Spoon lifting creamy protein overnight oats showing chia seed and oat texture
images.pinAlt: Tall jar of protein overnight oats with honey drizzling down the side and granola on top

storyIntro: Overnight oats are the easiest make-ahead breakfast there is, but most versions top out around 10g of protein. This one blends cottage cheese and Greek yogurt into the base before it even touches the oats, pushing it to 28g per jar without a scoop of powder.

Everything goes into the jar the night before — five minutes of prep, then the fridge does the work. By morning the oats have absorbed the liquid into something thick and pudding-like, ready for whatever toppings you're in the mood for.
storyNote: One thing worth knowing: the texture keeps improving the longer it sits, so if you can wait the full 8 hours instead of the minimum, it's worth it — the oats go from softened to genuinely creamy.
